title: OT/ICS Threat Intel - Power
description: Curated OT/ICS cybersecurity advisories, incidents and threat intelligence from primary sources. Editorial summaries by Mike Holcomb in partnership with UtilSec.

_otthreatfeed.usage: Free to reuse with attribution. Credit 'Mike Holcomb & UtilSec' and link back to otthreatfeed.com and to each item's linked primary source. Summaries are editorial paraphrase - verify against the primary source before acting.

items.title: SonicWall SMA 1000 zero-days under active attack — the remote-access boxes fronting utility engineer and vendor sessions
items.content_text: SonicWall says it has investigated multiple cases confirming active exploitation of two Secure Mobile Access 1000 flaws. CVE-2026-15409 is a CVSS 10.0 server-side request forgery in the Work Place interface, reachable by an unauthenticated remote attacker; CVE-2026-15410 (CVSS 7.2) lets an authenticated administrator inject OS commands through the Appliance Management Console. CISA added both to the KEV catalog on 14 July with a 17 July federal remediation deadline. For electric utilities the concern is placement rather than the bugs themselves: SMA appliances commonly terminate the vendor-maintenance and engineer sessions that reach EMS/SCADA, substation networks and the NERC CIP electronic security perimeter. An attacker who lands on the appliance sits astride that boundary with legitimate-looking access, so patching alone is insufficient — assume exposure and hunt.

_otthreatfeed.source_name: SonicWall PSIRT (SNWLID-2026-0008) / CISA KEV / The Hacker News
_otthreatfeed.date_note: SonicWall advisory & KEV addition 14 Jul 2026 — reporting 15 Jul; exploitation ongoing

items.title: Lantronix serial-to-IP converter flaw exploited in the wild — the quiet boxes bridging legacy substation serial gear
items.content_text: Attackers are exploiting CVE-2025-67038 in Lantronix serial-to-IP converters, one of the flaws disclosed in April as part of the BRIDGE:BREAK research project. Serial-to-IP converters are among the least-inventoried devices in a utility estate: they are frequently retrofitted to bring legacy relays, RTUs and meters onto Ethernet, then left in place for a decade without an owner, a patch process or a monitoring hook. That is exactly what makes them attractive — a converter typically sits inside the protected network with a clear path to the serial device it fronts, and compromise of the bridge means access to whatever is behind it. Exploitation is confirmed, and it follows an explicit OT threat warning, so treat this as an inventory problem before it is a patch problem.

_otthreatfeed.date_note: Disclosed Apr 2026 — exploitation reported 25 Jun 2026; ongoing

items.title: BeyondTrust patches critical auth-bypass flaws in Remote Support and PRA — the tooling utilities hand to vendors
items.content_text: BeyondTrust disclosed two authentication-bypass vulnerabilities on 7 July affecting Remote Support (RS) and Privileged Remote Access (PRA). CVE-2026-40138 (CVSS 9.2) stems from improper validation of authentication data and could let a network-positioned attacker bypass access controls and reach the appliance, including elevated accounts; CVE-2026-40139 involves improper processing of RS authentication requests. Both hinge on a specific authentication configuration being enabled, which limits the exposed population. Neither has been observed under active exploitation and no public proof-of-concept exists. Cloud customers were patched on 21 April; self-hosted deployments running RS or PRA 25.3.2 or earlier need action. Privileged remote access is the standing route vendors and integrators use into utility OT, which is why a 9.2 pre-auth bypass in it deserves attention even without exploitation.

items.title: July ICS Patch Tuesday: Siemens ships a CVSS 10 auth bypass fix, Rockwell patches critical DoS flaws in ControlLogix
items.content_text: The July 2026 cycle is a heavy one for grid operators. Siemens published nine advisories, six of them critical — headlined by a CVSS 10 token-invalidation flaw in Opencenter X that allows an attacker to bypass authentication and gain full application access, plus critical fixes in Simatic S7-1500, Mendix, Sidis Secured SmartPlug, Cadra and Desigo CC. Rockwell Automation published 12 advisories including three critical denial-of-service holes in CompactLogix, ControlLogix, Compact GuardLogix and GuardLogix controllers that can force a major non-recoverable fault — a controller-down condition, not a data-theft one. Schneider Electric fixed a high-severity arbitrary-code-execution issue in its IGSS SCADA product and an authentication bypass in EcoStruxure Cybersecurity Admin Expert. Germany’s VDE CERT added five more covering Murrelektronik, Mettler Toledo, Codesys and Wago.

_otthreatfeed.source_name: Siemens ProductCERT / Schneider / Rockwell PSIRT / SecurityWeek

items.title: CISA flags Schneider Easergy MiCOM Px40 protection relays and PowerChute Serial Shutdown
items.content_text: CISA’s 9 July batch of three ICS advisories was unusually energy-heavy: two of the three cover Schneider Electric products used in the energy sector — the Easergy MiCOM Px40 protection relay series and PowerChute Serial Shutdown — while the third covers OpenPLC v3, flagged for both water/wastewater and energy. The Px40 line matters most to transmission and distribution operators because protection relays sit at the sharp end of the grid: they are the devices that decide when to trip a circuit, and they are frequently reachable from substation automation networks and engineering laptops rather than from the internet. Consult the advisories for the specific CVEs, affected firmware ranges and Schneider’s recommended mitigations before planning relay work.

items.title: Hitachi Energy PROMOD V and e-mesh EMS, plus Siemens SINEC OS, headline CISA’s 7 July energy batch
items.content_text: CISA released seven ICS advisories on 7 July, five of which name products used in the energy sector: Hitachi Energy PROMOD V and Hitachi Energy e-mesh EMS, Siemens Mendix Studio Pro and Siemens SINEC OS, and Labcenter Proteus 9 (flagged for water/wastewater and energy). Digi International PortServer TS and Digi One SP IA9 serial servers, and the Hydro-Québec Le Circuit Électrique charging-station backend, round out the batch. The Hitachi Energy pair is the notable one for utilities — e-mesh EMS manages distributed energy and storage assets, while PROMOD V is planning software that typically holds detailed models of your network. Advisory coverage of planning and DER-management tooling is a reminder that grid risk is not confined to relays and RTUs.

items.title: CISA’s 14 July batch: two ABB advisories and a Rockwell EtherNet/IP adapter with an exposed debug port
items.content_text: CISA issued four ICS advisories on 14 July (ICSA-26-195-01 through -04), distributed alongside the vendors’ own Patch Tuesday releases. Three ABB advisories and one Rockwell advisory were pushed through CISA. The Rockwell entry covers the 1715-AENTR EtherNet/IP adapter, where a network-accessible debug port does not enforce proper privilege controls — allowing unauthenticated remote access to intrusive command-line functions. On the ABB side, advisories address Advant Master Online Builder (an incorrect Online Builder version shipped on installation media) and Ability Edgenius, where a Linux kernel privilege-escalation issue could let a locally authenticated user or a compromised container workload gain root. Consult each advisory for exact affected versions before acting.

items.title: NIST publishes an OT backup quick-start guide aimed at cutting recovery time after an incident
items.content_text: NIST released SP 1339, an OT Backup Quick Start Guide intended to improve industrial cyber resilience and accelerate incident recovery. The framing is worth noting for utilities: the guidance treats backup as an operational-recovery problem rather than a data-retention one. In grid environments the assets that actually determine restoration time are rarely the file servers — they are relay settings files, controller logic, EMS/SCADA configurations, historian baselines and the engineering-workstation images needed to push any of it back. Those are precisely the artifacts most often missing, stale, or stored on the same domain as the systems they would restore. The guide is short-format and pitched at getting a workable baseline in place, which makes it a reasonable prompt to test whether your OT restore actually works rather than whether the backup job reports success.

items.title: NERC CIP 2026 milestones keep tightening: low-impact controls, control-centre data protection, virtualization
items.content_text: The 2026 NERC CIP picture continues to move toward more prescriptive OT obligations. CIP-003-9, covering security management controls for lower-risk environments, took effect on 1 April 2026, and CIP-012-2 addresses the confidentiality and handling of real-time operational data exchanged between control centres. FERC has approved modifications improving baseline cybersecurity for low-impact BES Cyber Systems — including password requirements for remote users and intrusion detection for low-impact assets — alongside 11 updated CIP standards enabling the secure use of virtualization. A separate final rule directs NERC to address supply-chain risk in new or modified standards, extending coverage to certain network-connected equipment. The direction of travel is consistent: fewer exemptions for low-impact assets, and more explicit expectations for data in transit and virtualized infrastructure.

_otthreatfeed.date_note: CIP-003-9 effective 1 Apr 2026; further FERC-approved changes phasing in
